overlaps.description: why: I9 defines the feature-level FEATURE_PLANS cleanup behavior that must remain correct before I10 extends plan-block structure with Expected Behaviour; impact: if I10 lands first, confirm feature cleanup can miss newly introduced plan content or require immediate rework; action: finish and verify feature-section cleanup in confirm flow before updating planning contracts and lint around Expected Behaviour.

why: I10 and I11 both change planning-context contracts consumed by feature_commands, including how plan sections are parsed and how downstream planning workflows read intent versus dependency-index data; impact: changing index shape without aligning it to the new Expected Behaviour contract can create mismatched parser assumptions and noisy overlap-build inputs; action: keep parser and command updates aligned so Expected Behaviour remains plan-context input while ISSUE_DEP_INDEX stays a minimal normalized lookup artifact.
